original_image_tags: A single, aged, off-white paper document page. The paper has a torn upper right corner and a punched hole near the top edge. The majority of the page contains text that is extremely faint and illegible, appearing as ghosting or bleed-through from the reverse side. The text is mirrored and too faded to transcribe. A rectangular, brownish stain or tape residue covers the right-center portion of the page. At the bottom, several official stamps are visible, some oriented upside-down relative to the faint text above.

elements.description: Multiple stamped ink impressions in blue and purple on the back of a document. Visible text includes: 'RECEIVED MAY 19 10 31 AM '46', 'FBI INTERNAL SECURITY', 'RECEIVED MAY 20 10 06 AM '46', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE', and 'RECEIVED MAY 21 10 31 AM '46'. A torn corner at the top right reveals a dark background.

judge.reason: Description A states the stamp text is illegible and mirrored, while Description B quotes specific, clear, and distinct verbatim text from those stamps, creating a direct contradiction on the readability and content of the same visual elements.
